content:
Hi,
I'm planning to write some rust drivers for an embedded device(Pine Ox64). We have platform enablement patches based on linux v6.4-rc2.
I'm wondering on which branch/tag to rebase our patches on to get the most up-to-date rust kernel abstractions. Rust-for-Linux/linux:rust or Rust-for-Linux/linux tag rust-6.4?

See https://rust-for-linux.com/branches if everything that you need is already on mainline then use one of Linus' stable tags, otherwise use rust-next. If you still need some code that is not in rust-next, but in the rust branch, then there is still some work to be done, since many of the things left in that branch are not ready to be upstreamed. Feel free to tell us what you need and we will see how long it will take to upstream.
Hi, Thank you for the response. I'm trying to write a wifi driver first. So I would need things in the wireless subsystem, I don't think they're in either upstream or rust-next?
I tried rebasing linux 6.4-rc2 on rust which didn't go well. Is there an easy way to extract rust related changes from rust as a patchset?

Never mind, I was going about it the wrong way, looks like for my needs, git merge -Xours rust is enough. I see the project still doesn't have any abstractions for the wireless sybsystem. I will just directly use c bindings for now.
